rule: hit = kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; kw = STRICT substring count of multi-word content_words in title+snippet

results.reason: Source-domain Wikipedia page on qanat; no LLM/KV-cache mechanism, zero functional overlap with QANAT-KV architecture.
LMCache is an external cross-request KV cache layer, no single-anchor + fixed-slope decay + periodic eviction-merge design.
TRIM-KV per-token retention with scalar score decaying over time matches per-token decay aspect but lacks single anchor and fixed-slope passive (uses learned gate).
LKV uses differentiable learned head-wise budgets, antithetical to the passive fixed-slope no-learned-gating principle of QANAT-KV.
StreamingLLM fixed initial-token attention-sink anchors strongly match the single mother-well anchor concept though it lacks explicit fixed-slope decay and periodic eviction-merge.
Stateful multi-turn KV management with positional fidelity is unrelated to single-anchor passive-decay architecture.
H2O uses heavy-hitter oracle eviction which is data-driven attention-score based, not fixed-slope passive position decay with a single anchor.
Single attention-sink anchor + position-dependent decay weight for streaming KV is a near-identical functional match to QANAT-KV's mother-well anchor + fixed-slope token decay.

fail_reason: 3 functional hits found (ranks 3, 5, 8) — particularly rank 8 (2510.20194) implements anchor-sink + position-decay streaming cache, which is functionally near-identical to the proposed QANAT-KV single-anchor + fixed-slope passive decay architecture; the candidate is not novel.
